messages.content: ok so i'm officially done taking on new work for a while. had to turn down two inquiries this week which feels weird but also... necessary? my creative energy is like a battery that's been running on 5% for months
That sounds like a really healthy boundary to set. What's your current workload looking like?

just the indie game studio piece now, which is actually fun work. but vex... god, vex brewing is still hanging over me like a storm cloud. 5 weeks overdue at this point. every time i open that file i just freeze up
Five weeks overdue sounds really stressful. What's been making that project so difficult to finish?

scope creep from hell. started as one beer label, now they want three variants with different seasonal themes but same budget. and i said yes because i was desperate for the 800 bucks back in november. classic mistake
That's a tough spot. Have you been able to communicate with them about the scope changes?

barely. they keep sending these passive aggressive check-in emails. anyway i'm working on this scope-of-work template so this never happens again. like actual boundaries written down before any money changes hands
A solid template sounds like it could save you a lot of headaches. What are you including in it?

revision limits, deliverable specs, timeline with kill fees if they go radio silent. all the stuff i wish i'd had locked down before. the patreon growth has been good enough that i can actually afford to be picky now
That's great that the Patreon success is giving you more leverage. Sounds like you're setting yourself up much better for future projects.

exactly. like having only one active commission feels so spacious compared to juggling four back in november. i can actually breathe and think about my own work again instead of just putting out client fires
It sounds like you're finding a much healthier balance. Protecting that creative energy seems like the right call.

summary: Sam discusses declining new commissions to protect creative energy, dealing with the overdue Vex Brewing project that's 5 weeks late due to scope creep, and working on a scope-of-work template for future clients. Only has one active commission now with the indie game studio.
